top_signals.title: Tech Leads Stock Futures Higher; Trump Heads to NATO Summit | Bloomberg Brief 07/06/2026
top_signals.summary: US equity futures rise in a tech rebound as SK Hynix is set for a US listing this week. Oil slumps as flows through the Strait of Hormuz are restored after some tanker U-turns and after OPEC+ members agree to a modest rise in oil supply next month. Presidents Donald Trump and Volodymyr Zelenskyy are expected to meet at a NATO summit in Turkey. Anneka Treon of ING discusses the earnings environment and the impact of supply shocks in markets. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Return of the Right Sparks Record Inflow to Chile Peso Bonds
top_signals.summary: Foreign investors are piling into Chile’s local government debt at the fastest pace on record, attracted by a weaker peso, the prospect of interest rate cuts and the return of a more market-friendly government.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Europe’s Equity Bankers Bet on Capex Wave to Ride Out IPO Slump
top_signals.summary: Record spending on AI infrastructure and power grids is fueling a flurry of capital raises by listed companies, giving bankers one of their busiest starts in years and eclipsing the slowdown of initial public offerings in the region.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Super El Niño Could Trigger a Major Coal Boom in India
top_signals.summary: The super El Niño weather phenomenon this year will significantly boost India’s demand for coal-fired power generation over the next 12 months, as a generation gap could occur with higher temperatures, the Finland-based think tank Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA) said in a report on Monday. The El Niño, the recurrent weather pattern driving global temperatures higher, would affect most energy systems globally, but none would be as affected as India’s, according to CREA. The El Niño, typically associated…
